author: takeshy
description: Chat with local LLMs (Ollama, LM Studio) with local embeddings RAG, file encryption, edit history, slash commands, and workflow automation. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: takeshy
manifest.description: Chat with local LLMs (Ollama, LM Studio, AnythingLLM) with local embeddings RAG, file encryption, edit history, slash commands, and workflow automation.
manifest.name: Local LLM Hub

name: Local LLM Hub

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ### Improved Chat Onboarding - Added quick-start cards to empty chats. - Open the current dashboard or create a new one directly from chat. - Dashboard creation now asks for a name and safely handles duplicate names. - Added an “Ask about Local LLM Hub” action that enables the built-in help bundle and prepares a help question. ### Improved OKF Selection - Replaced always-visible OKF checkboxes with a compact add menu. - Active OKF bundles are displayed as removable chips. - Built-in help bundles are clearly identified in the selection menu. ### Secret Manager Improvements - Directories are now displayed before secret files at every hierarchy level. - Increased folder and expand/collapse icon sizes for better visibility. - Increased folder name size for easier scanning.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: fix: lint warning

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ### Secret Manager Dashboard Widget - Added a new **Secret Manager** dashboard widget for creating, searching, viewing, editing, and copying encrypted secrets from your vault. - Secrets are stored as individual `.encrypted` files and use the plugin’s existing encryption keys. - Supports searchable public metadata, descriptions, folder organization, unlock/copy actions, and encrypted editing from the dashboard. - Added Secret Manager settings, documentation, and screenshots across README translations. ### Reusable Kanban Board Files - Kanban dashboard widgets now use reusable `.kanban` board definition files under `Dashboards/Kanbans/`. - Multiple dashboards can share the same Kanban definition while keeping card order local to each widget. - Kanban settings now support selecting/editing shared board files. - Existing inline Kanban widget definitions are migrated automatically when dashboards are opened. - Kanban cards can now show additional frontmatter fields, including computed file fields such as `file.name`, `file.path`, and `file.content`. - `file.content` display can be capped with a maximum character length, so cards can show a controlled preview of the note body. ### Encrypted File Improvements - Improved encrypted file metadata support so descriptions and public metadata can be stored outside the ciphertext for search/listing while keeping secret values encrypted. - Added tests around encrypted file metadata handling. ### Built-in Help - Added the Local LLM Hub help documentation as a built-in OKF knowledge bundle. - The help bundle is available directly from the chat OKF selector without external configuration. - Documentation is regenerated automatically during development and production builds. - Includes documentation for Secret Manager and reusable Kanban boards. ### MCP Improvements - Disabling all MCP servers in chat now correctly prevents MCP tools from being exposed to the model. - Explicitly disabled MCP servers remain disabled after settings refreshes. - Newly connected MCP servers are enabled initially and can then be controlled individually.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

  - Added a dashboard switcher to the dashboard toolbar.
    - Click the dashboard title to switch between `.dashboard` files in the vault.
    - Dashboards with duplicate names show their full path for easier identification.
    - The current dashboard is marked in the switcher menu.

  - Removed the dedicated Markdown dashboard widget.
    - Existing `type: markdown` dashboard widgets are now treated as File widgets.
    - Markdown files remain supported through the File widget.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Added

  - Added File widget memo support for Dashboard.
    - Attach memos to source files from Dashboard file views.
    - Select text and use **Add to memo** to save the selected text as a reference quote.
    - Memo quotes can be clicked to jump back to the referenced text where possible.
  - Added Memo List widget for browsing files with dashboard memos.
  - Added PDF/EPUB-related viewer support used by the File widget.
  - Added dashboard layout equalize controls and tests.

### Changed

  - Dashboard no longer uses a separate **Edit / Done** toggle.
    - Widget add, undo/redo, and layout alignment controls are always available.
    - Widget settings/maximize buttons are always visible.
    - Drag/resize handles now match the compact upstream behavior.
  - Updated File widget header, memo button alignment, and selection context menu styling to match `obsidian-llm-hub`.
  - Reorganized docs into the OKF help structure and removed older standalone docs.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Decouple Node API types from @types/node for plugin review

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Maintenance release: enforce stricter TypeScript lint rules (no user-facing changes).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed

  - Fixed OKF bundle selections resetting to all enabled on startup.
      - OKF bundle ON/OFF state is now persisted in plugin settings.
      - Previously saved selections are restored after restart.
      - Turning all OKF bundles off is now preserved instead of being treated as “select all”.

  - Cleaned up TypeScript unsafe-value lint warnings in core provider, MCP, OKF, PDF, RAG, dashboard, and chat history
    code paths.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ### New - Added external skill installation from the official takeshy/llm-hub-skills repository. - Added an External skills settings section with catalog loading, install, installed-skill listing, and update checks. - Added OKF knowledge source support. - Added OKF bundle discovery from the configured knowledge directory. - Added an OKF selector in chat so discovered bundles can be enabled per chat. - Added support for host-specific skill patches via unified diff files listed in manifest.json. - Added documentation for external skills and OKF knowledge sources.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed
- Fixed Timeline post editing wikilink completion ([[...]]) being hidden behind long edited posts.
- The suggestion list now appears near the cursor while editing existing Timeline posts, including when editing text near the beginning of a long post.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed
  - Fixed encrypted files (`.encrypted`) not opening in the dedicated encrypted file view.
  - `.encrypted` files are now registered with Obsidian and open directly in the decrypt/edit UI.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:

## New Contributors

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: fix lint warning

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## New - Added Dashboard support with .dashboard files. - Added a responsive widget grid with edit/view modes, drag, resize, undo/redo, and auto-save. - Added dashboard widgets: - Base widget for Obsidian Bases views - Markdown note embed - Web iframe embed - Workflow output widget with sidecar cache - Kanban board backed by note frontmatter - Added LLM Hub: Create dashboard command. - Added built-in dashboard agent skill for creating dashboards and backing .base files from chat. ### Dashboard Workflow Widgets - Workflow widgets now render from a hidden sidecar cache instead of running on every render. - Added manual run, stale-on-open auto-refresh, and periodic refresh while the dashboard is open. - Cached error states are saved so failed runs are visible in the widget. ### Kanban - Added frontmatter-driven Kanban boards. - Cards can be dragged between columns to update status. - Added new-card creation that writes matching folder/tag/status metadata. - Added note preview modal and open-note action.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Combined RAG

  - Restrict source candidates for **Combine internal** RAG settings.
  - Only standalone internal RAG settings with the same effective embedding server URL and embedding model can now be selected
  together.
  - This prevents accidentally combining indexes built with incompatible embedding configurations.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved RAG sync resilience

- Process changed files in smaller file batches during RAG sync.
- Save progress between batches so large first-time indexes can recover more easily if Obsidian crashes.
- Continue syncing remaining files automatically from a single Sync action.

### PDF handling

- If PDF text extraction fails, the failed PDF paths are shown after sync.
- Failed PDFs are saved by checksum and shown in the indexed file list as `0 chunks`.
- Failed PDFs are not retried on later syncs unless the PDF file changes.
- To force re-import, rename the PDF, modify the file, or clear and rebuild the RAG index.

### Documentation

- Added README notes about batch sync behavior, failed PDF handling, and re-import options.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved RAG indexing

- Improved large-vault RAG sync behavior.
  - PDF files now use file-level checksums, reducing repeated PDF text extraction after migration.
  - Existing PDF indexes are migrated without re-embedding unchanged PDF chunks when possible.
- Improved RAG sync progress reporting.
  - Sync progress now distinguishes scanning, embedding, and saving phases.
  - Ollama embedding batches are smaller to avoid long stalls during initial indexing.

### RAG index modes

- Added an **Index mode** selector for RAG settings:
  - **Internal**: build and sync an index from vault folders.
  - **Combine internal**: combine multiple existing internal RAG settings and search them as one.
  - **External**: load one or more external index directories.
- Combined internal RAG settings can be selected from the UI.
- External index paths can be entered one per line.
- Compatible indexes are merged at search ti

### Ollama embedding fixes

- Fixed embedding model fetching when the embedding server URL is `http://localhost:11434` or `http://127.0.0.1:11434`.
  - These URLs now use Ollama’s `/api/tags` endpoint.
  - Embedding-only models such as `nomic-embed-text` are detected correctly.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixed
- Fixed UI and workflow failures caused by using Obsidian’s createEl / createDiv / createSpan helpers on
    activeDocument.
- Replaced those calls with activeDocument.createElement(...) to avoid invalid direct append operations on Document.

## New Contributors

Thanks to @OlivierLmr for their first contribution in #10, which fixed workflow and modal UI rendering issues related  to `activeDocument` element creation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed

  - Removed the bundled React DOM 19 code path that triggered Obsidian's dynamic `<script>` element scanner warning.
    - Downgraded `react` and `react-dom` to React 18.
    - Kept existing React view mounting behavior via `createRoot`.
    - Confirmed the production bundle no longer contains `createElement("script")`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Maintenance

- Fixed CSS lint issues reported during plugin review.
    - Restored malformed CSS section comments that caused parse errors.
    - Removed duplicate CSS declarations and duplicate selector blocks.
    - Replaced unsupported dotted `text-decoration` shorthand with a compatible style.
    - Removed broad `:has()` selectors and unnecessary `!important` usage.

- Addressed additional Obsidian plugin review lint follow-ups.
    - Standardized timer/window API usage.
    - Adjusted binary buffer handling for RAG and workflow file writes.
    - Fixed a stylesheet block closure issue.

- Added GitHub release asset provenance attestation for release builds.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed
  - Added GitHub artifact attestations for release assets.
  - Release workflow now generates provenance attestations for:
    - `main.js`
    - `manifest.json`
    - `styles.css`

### Changed
  - Updated release workflow permissions to allow OIDC and artifact attestation generation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixed
  - Fixed CSS syntax error caused by an unclosed block in `styles.css`.
  - Updated timer calls to use `window.setTimeout`, `window.clearTimeout`, `window.setInterval`, and  `window.clearInterval` to match plugin review guidance.
  - Updated `requestAnimationFrame` usage to `window.requestAnimationFrame`.
  - Removed unnecessary `ArrayBuffer` assertions by explicitly copying binary data into new `ArrayBuffer` instances.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Changed
  - Addressed Obsidian plugin review findings across source and UI code.
  - Improved popout window compatibility by using `activeWindow` / `activeDocument` where appropriate.
  - Replaced direct static style assignments with CSS classes for better theming and maintainability.
  - Replaced deprecated UTF-8 base64 handling with `TextEncoder` / `TextDecoder`.
  - Removed the unused `builtin-modules` development dependency.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved
- Vault tools now support text-based vault files beyond Markdown, including `.canvas`, `.json`, `.txt`, `.csv`, `.yaml`, `.yml`,`.svg`, `.html`, `.xml`, `.css`, `.js`, `.ts`, and `.tsx`.
- `create_note`, `read_note`, `propose_edit`, `rename_note`, `list_notes`, and `search_notes` can now work with supported non-  Markdown files such as Obsidian Canvas files.
- When no extension is provided, vault tools continue to default to `.md` for compatibility with existing note workflows.
- Extensionless lookups now prefer `.md` files when multiple files share the same basename, while explicit paths like `Plan.canvas`  still resolve to the requested non-Markdown file.

### Fixed
- `create_note` no longer prepends Markdown tags to non-Markdown files, preventing invalid JSON when creating `.canvas` or `.json`  files.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features

- Clickable tool tags in chat — Tool names shown under "Tools used" (read_note, create_note, update_note, propose_edit, rename_note, get_active_note) are now clickable and open the referenced note directly. Tools that don't  target a specific note (search_notes, list_notes, MCP tools, etc.) show their argument details in a Notice on click

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
🐛 Bug Fixes

@-mention resolution now handles all vault file paths

Fixed a class of bugs where mentioning a note via ＠path in Chat or the AI Workflow modal silently failed or produced wrong results.

### What was broken

- Paths with spaces — @My Notes/Daily.md resolved only My and left the rest as literal text.
- Unicode paths — @メモ/日本語.md often failed to match depending on surrounding characters.
- Regex-special characters — paths containing (, ), +, ., [, etc. were silently truncated.
- False-positive splicing — if only foo.md existed in the vault, text like seefoo.md or @foo.md/child would splice foo.md's content into the middle of an unrelated token.
- Ambiguous suffixes — when both a.md and folder/a.md existed, @folder/a.md could get partially matched on the shorter suffix.

### What's fixed

All of the above. Mention resolution now scans the actual vault file list (longest path first), matches each path literally, and requires clean token boundaries.

Additionally, when the same file is mentioned multiple times in one prompt, it is now read once instead of once per mention.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🔧 1 File = 1 Workflow — breaking change

Each `.md` file now holds **exactly one** workflow code block. The name inside the workflow YAML is no longer an addressable identifier — a workflow is identified by its file path, period.

### What breaks

- **Multi-block files become read-only.** Opening a file with ≥2 workflow blocks shows an error banner with a new **Split into individual files** button that migrates blocks 2..N into sibling files (block 1 stays in place).
- **Sub-workflow `name:` property removed.** The workflow node now takes only `path:` — each file resolves unambiguously to one workflow.
- **Skill capability `name:` field removed.** `run_skill_workflow` IDs are derived from `path` alone (`.md` stripped, `/` → `_`).
- **Event trigger / hotkey IDs are now bare paths** (no more `path#workflowName`).

### Auto-migration

- Persisted `path#name` hotkey + event trigger IDs are stripped on first plugin load (dedups colliding entries).
- Old Obsidian-side hotkey commands with the legacy hash stay registered until plugin reload, but no new legacy commands are created.

### Panel UX

- The in-file workflow dropdown is gone — the header shows the file's basename as the workflow name.
- **New Workflow (AI)** no longer defaults the output path to the currently-open file (it would always collide); falls back to `workflows/{{name}}`.
- Creating an AI workflow whose target path already holds a workflow is rejected up-front instead of silently appending.

## 🐛 Bug fixes (since 0.11.0)

- **Vault skills stay usable when `Vault tools: none`.** `read_note` is kept available whenever any vault skill is active — without it the LLM can neither fetch SKILL.md nor construct a valid `run_skill_workflow` call.
- **Inline tool-call JSON is stripped from visible chat output** (#9). Small local models sometimes emit `{"tool": "...", "arguments": {...}}` as plain text inside their response; the dispatcher now parses and executes those calls, and the JSON blob no longer leaks into the rendered reply.
- **`get_workflow_spec` accepts bare-name `nodeTypes`**. Some models emit `"command"` or `"command, http"` instead of `["command", "http"]`; all three forms now work, restoring the token-saving purpose of the tool.

## 🧹 Internals

- New pure helpers with unit tests: `workflowMigration.ts` (legacy ID stripper) and `multiBlockMigration.ts` (file splitter).
- `extractInputVariables` excludes any `_`-prefixed name (matches the system-variable convention documented in the AI workflow spec), replacing the previous hand-maintained allow-list.
- `extractCapabilitiesBlock` warns once per skill path when the `skill-capabilities` YAML block fails to parse, so authors can see typos instead of silently missing capabilities.
- `syncSkillInputVariables` is gated on `skills/` prefix — a stray SKILL.md elsewhere in the vault no longer triggers capability rewrites.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## ✨ Confirm Modal Overhaul

### Rich diff view
  - **Unified / Split view toggle**
  - **Word-level highlighting** — changed parts are highlighted per word
  - **Per-line comments** — click any line to attach a comment; comments are sent to the LLM as structured feedback (`Line 12 (+):
  <content>\nComment: ...`) when you click "Request changes"

### Skill-modify visibility
  - **SKILL.md instructions diff now shown** alongside the YAML diff when modifying a skill. Previously only the workflow YAML diff was
  visible, hiding whether the AI actually updated the SKILL.md body
  - **"No changes" indicator** on sections where old === new — distinguishes "AI returned same content" from "AI returned nothing"
  - **Clearer section labels**: "Changes" → "Workflow YAML changes" / "SKILL.md instructions changes"

### Confirm modal UX
  - **Scrollable middle region** keeps the feedback textarea and action buttons pinned at the bottom
  - **"Your request" section** displays the original request read-only at the top
  - **Additional Request textarea starts empty** (no more pre-populated previous request)
  - **"Request changes" button starts disabled**, enables when the user types or attaches a line comment
  - **Copy button on AI explanation**

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## ✨ New Features ### AI Workflow / Skill Generation Overhaul - **Three-phase pipeline** (Plan → Generate → Review) for better quality - Plan: plain-language plan shown first (OK / Re-plan / Cancel) - Review: auto-review after generation; pick OK (with confirmation) / Refine / Cancel - Refine feeds review feedback back to the LLM for another pass - **Split Create buttons**: dedicated "Create workflow with AI" and "Create skill with AI" entry points - **Modify skill with AI**: when a `SKILL.md` is active, edit both the instructions body AND the referenced workflow file in one pass (frontmatter preserved) - **Auto-repair for invalid YAML**: on parse failure, re-prompts the LLM with the error message up to 2 times - **Parse-failure recovery view**: keeps the modal open with raw output + copy button if auto-repair still fails ### Chat UX - **Clickable skill chips**: chips in the input area and assistant badges open their `SKILL.md` (built-ins are static labels) - **Failed workflow recovery**: "Open workflow" button on failed tool calls opens the file and switches to the Workflow / skill tab ### UI polish - "Workflow" tab renamed to **"Workflow / skill"** - `SKILL.md` header now exposes "Create skill with AI" alongside "Modify skill with AI" - Preview modal: plan / thinking / review sections collapsed by default - Thinking panel hidden until the model actually emits reasoning content ## 🔧 Improvements - **Surface LM Studio / llama.cpp context-overflow errors** (previously swallowed as empty HTTP 200) - Workflow spec clarifications: optional `variable.value`, `:json` interpolation semantics, bare-name `json.source` - Plan / Review prompts written in the user's configured language - `run_skill_workflow` failures include the workflow path and a don't-auto-retry instruction - Variable nodes with omitted `value` now preserve existing values (acts as an input declaration)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Chat view restores previous thread — Reopening the Chat view within the same Obsidian session now automatically restores the last active  conversation. No more losing your place when switching between tabs.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fix

  - Fix PDF page offset calculation — Empty pages in PDFs no longer cause incorrect page labels. Previously, pages with no extractable text would cause all subsequent pages to share the same offset, leading to inaccurate pages X-Y of N labels in search results.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## PDF support for RAG indexing and search PDF files in your vault are now automatically indexed alongside Markdown files. Text is extracted using Obsidian's built-in PDF.js, chunked, and embedded just like regular notes. - PDF indexing — PDF text extraction and embedding during RAG sync - Page tracking — Each chunk stores its page range (e.g. "pages 2-5 of 24"), displayed as a badge in search results - PDF preview — Switch between extracted text view and PDF page preview per result - Resilient sync — If PDF extraction fails temporarily (encrypted PDF, read error), existing indexed chunks are preserved - File extension filter — Filter search results by file type (e.g. md, pdf) ### Improved keyword search - Quoted phrases — Use "MCP Apps" to match exact phrases in keyword filter - CJK matching — Whitespace normalization and space-stripped matching for CJK text split by PDF extraction artifacts (e.g. searching "3つのコア機能" matches "3 つのコア機能") - AI keyword suggestion — Now includes English translations for non-English input; redundant terms (containing original keywords as substrings) are automatically removed - Top K limit — Increased from 200 to 999 ### Other - Sync progress display during RAG indexing

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fix: plugin update now works correctly

### Fixes

- Fixed missing `version` field in `manifest.json` that prevented Obsidian from detecting and applying updates (#8)
- Cleaned up invalid entries in `versions.json`

### Prevention

- `version-bump.mjs` now falls back to reading `package.json` when `npm_package_version` is not set
- Added `manifest.json` version validation step to CI (build & release workflows)

### Upgrade note

- **From 0.8.0 or earlier:** update to 0.9.2 should work normally.
- **From 0.9.0 or 0.9.1:**
  1. Uninstall the plugin from Community Plugins
  2. Press the update button in BRAT
  3. Re-enable the plugin from Community Plugins

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## RAG Search: Multi-field keyword filter with AI suggestion ### Multi-field AND/OR keyword filter - Keyword filter now supports multiple fields - Within a field: space-separated terms use OR logic (any term matches) - Between fields: AND logic (all fields must match) - Click + AND to add a filter field, ✕ to remove ### AI keyword suggestion - Each filter field has a ✦ (sparkle) button - Uses the configured AI refine model to expand keywords with synonyms and related terms - Click ↩ to restore original keywords ### Other changes - Increased Top K maximum from 50 to 200

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## RAG Search enhancements ### Keyword Filter - Search results can now be narrowed down by keyword (space-separated AND logic) - Matches against both chunk text and file path - "Select all" and result count reflect the filtered view ### Chunk Editor - Click the pencil icon on an expanded result to open the chunk editor modal - Load previous/next chunks from the same file with automatic overlap removal - Edited results show an "Edited" badge and accent border in the results list - AI-refined chunks open as text-only editor on reopen; manually edited chunks retain the full interface ### AI Refine - "Refine with AI" button in the chunk editor automatically expands context and cleans up text using your local LLM - Phase 1: Loads adjacent chunks in parallel, then AI evaluates if more context is needed (up to 5 iterations) - Phase 2: LLM streams refined text into the editor, removing chunking artifacts while preserving all meaningful content - Select the refine model in search settings (gear icon) from your available local LLM models

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ## New Features - Built-in Obsidian Skills - Three built-in skills that teach the LLM about Obsidian-specific file formats - obsidian-markdown - Wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian Flavored Markdown syntax - json-canvas - Creating and editing .canvas files with nodes, edges, and groups - obsidian-bases - Creating and editing .base files with views, filters, formulas, and summaries - Built-in skills are shown with a "built-in" badge in the skill dropdown - Not active by default; enable manually from the skill selector

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix: sentence case lint error in placeholder text

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## RAG Search Tab — Index Settings Panel

Added an index settings panel (gear icon) to the RAG Search tab.

  - Adjust Chunk Size and Chunk Overlap via sliders directly from the search tab
  - Edit Target Folders and Excluded Patterns without leaving the search UI
  - Sync button with cancel support — rebuild the index without navigating to plugin settings
  - Last synced timestamp display
  - Indexed files list accordion with per-file chunk counts
  - Changing chunk size or overlap now triggers a full re-index on next sync

## Fix: RAG search results not sent to LLM

Fixed an issue where text attachments from RAG Search results were not being passed to the LLM when sent to Chat.

  - Text attachments are now base64-decoded and included in the message content sent to the LLM
  - Stored as llmContent in chat history, so the LLM can reference past attachment content across conversation turns and after history reload

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New: RAG Search tab

A dedicated RAG Search tab has been added between Chat and Workflow, enabling standalone semantic search across your indexed vault notes.

### Features

- RAG setting selector — Choose which named RAG setting to search against
- Adjustable parameters — Override Top K and minimum score threshold per search
- Result list with preview — Each result shows file path, relevance score (%), and expandable text preview
- Batch selection — Select all / individual results with checkboxes
- Send to Chat — Selected results are attached as editable text to the Chat tab, with RAG auto-cleared to prevent duplicate context injection
- File navigation — Click a result's file path to open it in Obsidian
- Editable attachments — Text attachments from RAG search are clickable in the input area to view/edit content in a modal before sending
- Empty state guidance — When no RAG settings exist, shows a setup guide with a link to plugin settings

### How it works

1. Open the Rag search tab
2. Select a RAG setting and enter a query
3. Review results, select the relevant ones
4. Click Chat with selected — switches to Chat with results pre-attached

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Multiple Named RAG Settings

You can now create and manage multiple RAG settings. Configure different embedding models, chunk sizes, target folders, and more for each setting, and switch between them on the fly.

### Key Changes:

  - Multiple RAG settings — Create, rename, and delete named RAG settings from the settings page
  - RAG selector in chat — A dropdown next to the model selector lets you choose which RAG setting to use per conversation
  - External index toggle — Replaced the text field with a toggle switch; the path input only appears when enabled
  - Automatic migration — Existing ragConfig is automatically migrated to a "Default" named setting, including on-disk index data

### Internal Improvements:

  - RAG indexes are now stored per setting under LocalLlmHub/rag/<name>/
  - Workspace state persisted in workspace-state.json
  - Setting name sanitization with directory collision detection
  - Fixed shallow-copy bug in DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_STATE initialization
  - Fixed missing dropdown arrow indicator in chat UI selectors

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Improvements

- Renamed framework label to "LM Studio (OpenAI compatible)" to clarify it works with any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint
- Embedding model fetch now filters by name pattern, excluding non-embedding models

## Documentation

- Added cloud LLM setup guide (OpenAI, Google Gemini) with RAG configuration instructions

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Breaking Change

- Renamed workflow code block language from ```workflow to ```llm-workflow
  - Existing ```workflow blocks can still be read and executed
  - New and saved workflows will use ```llm-workflow

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed inability to enable this plugin alongside Gemini Helper
  - Both plugins registered a code block processor for the workflow language, causing the second one to fail on load

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

  - Fix chat terminating immediately when using tools (Vault/MCP) with AnythingLLM
      - AnythingLLM's OpenAI-compatible endpoint does not support the tools parameter — sending it caused the server to return an internal chat ID instead of LLM output
      - Tools are now automatically disabled for AnythingLLM with a notice shown to the user
  - Fix pending tool calls lost on stream end
      - When a stream ended without a [DONE] marker, accumulated tool calls were silently dropped
      - Added support for legacy finish_reason: "function_call"

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features

- vLLM support: Added vLLM as a selectable LLM framework with default URL http://localhost:8000
- Separate embedding server URL: RAG settings now allow specifying a dedicated embedding server URL, independent from the LLM server
- Event-triggered workflows: Workflows can be automatically triggered by vault events (create, modify, delete, rename, file-open) with built-in event variables (_eventType, _eventFilePath, _eventFile, _eventFileContent, _eventOldPath)

## Improvements

- Introduced event variable alias system for backward compatibility (_eventFile and __eventFile__ both supported)
- Refactored RAG settings with updateRagConfig helper to reduce repetition
- Added event-triggered workflow documentation and examples to the workflow spec

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed ESLint errors (no-base-to-string on unknown types, sentence-case on URL placeholder)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes
 - Fix Mermaid diagram rendering on mobile: Fixed an issue where workflow Mermaid diagrams failed to render on mobile. Mermaid's \<br\> tags inside foreignObject caused strict XML parsing to fail in mobile WebView. Switched to HTML parsing to tolerate HTML void elements.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug fixes
- Fix streaming stalls — Replaced the stream signaling mechanism with a robust version-counter pattern (StreamSignal) that prevents lost  notifications between Node.js event callbacks and the async generator. Added a 2-minute idle timeout so stalled connections are detected and  reported as errors instead of hanging indefinitely.

## Changes

  - Workspace folder is now fixed — The workspace folder name (LocalLlmHub) is no longer configurable and is treated as a constant, consistent with the skills and workflows folders. The setting has been removed from the settings UI. 
  - Fix sentence case for UI text — Addressed Obsidian plugin review feedback for sentence case compliance in settings labels.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes

  - Workspace folder is now fixed — The workspace folder name (LocalLlmHub) is no longer configurable and is treated as a constant, consistent with the skills and workflows folders. The setting has been removed from the settings UI. Existing saved settings will be automatically migrated.
  - Fix sentence case for UI text — Addressed Obsidian plugin review feedback for sentence case compliance in settings labels.

## Migration notes

  - If you had customized the workspace folder name to something other than LocalLlmHub, your chat history and RAG data will remain in the old folder. You can manually move the contents to LocalLlmHub to restore access.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

  - Mermaid diagram: Fix parse error when workflow nodes target "end" (Mermaid reserved word) — now routes to FINISH node with display label  kept as "■ END"
  - Clipboard copy: Replace deprecated document.execCommand("copy") with ClipboardItem API fallback
  - SVG rendering: Use DOMParser instead of direct innerHTML assignment for Mermaid diagram rendering, with parse error handling
  - Code quality: Remove eslint-disable for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any by using typed Record<string, unknown> cast
  - UI text: Use sentence case for event trigger labels ("File created" instead of "File Created")

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

  - Mermaid diagram: Fix parse error when workflow nodes target "end" (Mermaid reserved word) — now routes to FINISH node with display label  kept as "■ END"
  - Clipboard copy: Replace deprecated document.execCommand("copy") with ClipboardItem API fallback
  - SVG rendering: Use DOMParser instead of direct innerHTML assignment for Mermaid diagram rendering, with parse error handling
  - Code quality: Remove eslint-disable for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any by using typed Record<string, unknown> cast
  - UI text: Use sentence case for event trigger labels ("File created" instead of "File Created")

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Feature

- rag-sync: single file sync — The rag-sync workflow node now supports syncing a single file when path is specified, making it significantly faster for event-triggered workflows. Use oldPath to handle file renames. Omitting path falls back to a full sync. Unchanged files are automatically skipped via checksum comparison.

```
  - id: sync
    type: rag-sync
    path: "{{__eventFilePath__}}"
    saveTo: syncResult
```

## Documentation

  - Add AnythingLLM as a supported framework across all docs (README, README_ja, manifest, framework comparison table)
  - Fix workflow node category count from 9 to 12
  - Add execute_javascript to the vault tools list
  - Fix workflowSpec inaccuracies: folder-list output format, remove non-existent prompt-selection title property
  - Update WORKFLOW_NODES docs (EN/JA) with both single-file and full sync modes for rag-sync

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes

- Fixed skills/workflows folder paths — skills and workflows folders are now fixed constants instead of configurable settings. The skills folder setting has been removed from the settings UI.
- File explorer icons — 🧩 and ⚡ emoji icons are now shown before the skills and workflows folders in Obsidian's file explorer.

releases.author: takeshy
releases.description:
## Changes
- Add AnythingLLM support (OpenAI-compatible API with /v1/openai path prefix)
- Fix SSE streaming bug where empty `usage: {}` caused premature stream termination
- Fix embedding model settings UI (remove redundant text input, add placeholder)
- LLM Modal: re-render on framework change, AnythingLLM-specific API key description

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/takeshy/obsidian-local-llm-hub/compare/0.2.1...0.2.2
